statement.markdown:
Given a singly linked list, reverse it in-place.

Example Input:

5 -> 4 -> 3 -> 2 -> 1 -> None

Example Output:

1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5 -> None
statement.inputFormat: A singly linked list node class and an instance of the linked list
statement.notes: The input linked list is a valid representation of an integer linked list, and the output should be the same representation in reverse order.

hints.text: Iterate through the linked list and reverse each node

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The idea is to reverse each node in the list and return the new head.
editorial.bruteForce: Naive approach is to create a new linked list and add each node to it in reverse order
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Iterate through the linked list and reverse each node, returning the new head
